name: emergency-financial-assistance
description: One-time emergency funds for rent, utilities, and essential expenses. How to find money by Friday. The bridge between crisis and longer-term benefits.
source: Liberation Labs mutual aid knowledge base

triples.subject: 211 helpline
triples.object: local emergency assistance funds, utility help, food banks, and shelter — dial 2-1-1 or visit 211.org

triples.subject: emergency assistance application
triples.object: proof of crisis (shutoff notice, eviction filing, medical bill), proof of income, and ID

triples.subject: TANF emergency assistance
triples.object: one-time cash payment in some states for families with children facing housing or utility emergencies

triples.subject: LIHEAP
triples.object: Low Income Home Energy Assistance — pays heating/cooling bills directly, available seasonally through local agencies

triples.subject: utility shutoff
triples.object: contacting the utility company to request a payment plan BEFORE the shutoff date — many states require this option

triples.subject: medical debt
triples.object: accessing emergency rooms — EMTALA requires treatment regardless of ability to pay, and hospital financial assistance (charity care) exists

triples.subject: hospital financial assistance
triples.object: nonprofit hospitals (most hospitals) under IRS rules — apply even after receiving a bill

triples.subject: prescription assistance
triples.object: manufacturer patient assistance programs, GoodRx/RxAssist for cost reduction, and state pharmaceutical assistance programs

triples.subject: SNAP emergency benefits
triples.object: within 7 days if household has less than $150 monthly income and less than $100 in liquid resources

triples.subject: eviction prevention fund
triples.object: most counties — administered through local community action agencies, typically covers 1-3 months rent

triples.subject: deposit assistance
triples.object: local housing authorities, community action agencies, or transitional housing programs

triples.subject: car repair assistance
triples.object: some community action agencies, Modest Needs, and local auto repair charity programs

triples.subject: modest needs self-sufficiency grant
triples.object: one-time grants to prevent specific financial crises for people above poverty line but below self-sufficiency

triples.subject: food stamps (SNAP)
triples.object: regular processing within 30 days, expedited within 7 days for emergencies — apply at local DSS or online

triples.subject: WIC (Women Infants Children)
triples.object: supplemental food, nutrition education, and healthcare referrals for pregnant women, new mothers, and children under 5

triples.subject: free tax preparation
triples.object: VITA (Volunteer Income Tax Assistance) sites for households under ~$67,000 — may reveal unclaimed credits worth thousands

triples.subject: Earned Income Tax Credit
triples.object: refundable tax credit up to ~$7,800 for working families — can be claimed for prior years if missed

triples.subject: mutual aid network
triples.object: community-organized direct assistance without means testing or bureaucracy — find local groups through social media or community boards

triples.subject: benefit enrollment
triples.object: Benefits.gov screening tool, local community action agencies, and hospital social workers who can identify all available programs

triples.subject: garnishment protection
triples.object: Social Security, SSI, VA benefits, and federal student aid from most creditor garnishment — these funds are protected in bank accounts for 2 months

triples.subject: internet/phone assistance
triples.object: Affordable Connectivity Program (ACP) for $30/month internet subsidy, Lifeline for $9.25/month phone/internet discount

triples.subject: emergency food
triples.object: food banks (no proof of income required at most), community meal programs, and school meal programs for children

triples.subject: veteran emergency assistance
triples.object: VA's Supportive Services for Veteran Families (SSVF) — rapid re-housing and homelessness prevention for veterans

triples.subject: crowdfunding
triples.object: GoFundMe and similar platforms can help but may affect benefits eligibility — check before posting if receiving means-tested benefits

triples.subject: financial crisis triage
triples.object: 1) immediate safety (shelter, food, medication) 2) prevent irreversible loss (eviction, utility shutoff) 3) stabilize (benefits enrollment, payment plans)
